name: OmopCondition
title: OMOP-Convertible Condition → condition_occurrence
description: FHIR Condition that can be converted to OMOP `condition_occurrence`. Excludes entered-in-error; requires a subject, onset, and a code in the OMOP Condition domain.

binding.description: FHIR disease-state machine (active/recurrence/relapse/inactive/remission/resolved). Carried verbatim to condition_status_source_value and mapped to condition_status_concept_id via cm.fhir_clinical_status_to_omop (only 'resolved'→32906 has a clean OMOP equivalent).

Preferred input for condition_status_concept_id — semantically closer to OMOP's diagnostic-confidence axis than clinicalStatus. Maps via cm.fhir_verification_status_to_omop ('confirmed'→32893, 'provisional'→32899). Must not be entered-in-error.
Maps to condition_type_concept_id via cm.fhir_condition_category_to_omop (encounter-diagnosis→32827, problem-list-item→32840, health-concern→32817). Defaults to 32817 'EHR' when absent.
Coded diagnosis. Source priority SNOMED → ICD10CM → ICD9CM → ICD10 (deterministic, not coding.first()). Standard concept resolved via vocab.concept_relationship 'Maps to' with domain_id='Condition'.

element.comment: → visit_occurrence_id (hash of encounter UUID). NULL when absent.

Primary source for condition_start_date/datetime. dateTime + Period branches both supported; Period.start used when only Period is present. Falls back to Condition.recordedDate when both onset branches are missing.
→ condition_end_date/datetime. dateTime + Period.end branches; abatement.string carried into stop_reason.
Last-resort fallback for condition_start_date when onset is absent. Per HL7 FHIR-OMOP IG, using recorded date as proxy for onset introduces analytical bias — document on a per-source-system basis.

Supporting clinical findings. SNOMED/ICD10CM codes inside evidence.concept.coding act as last-resort fallback (priorities 5–6 in stage-2 UNION ALL) for condition_concept_id when Condition.code has no resolvable coding. Synthea omits this field.
